compiled_state.title: As a Ruby or Node/JS developer, architect-foundation pre-fills commands.* config keys with stack-appropriate defaults
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Make architect-foundation stack-aware by reading the detected stack and pre-filling commands.test_runner, commands.lint, commands.format, and commands.format_check in the generated dso-config.conf with sensible defaults derived from the same routing tables that Epics A and B establish. Update architect-foundation/SKILL.md and CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md with a per-stack reference table. Implement re-run preservation (emit [DSO INFO] when a key is already set).

**Why**: Today, architect-foundation writes empty placeholders for all commands.* keys regardless of stack. Ruby and Node/JS developers must manually look up CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md and fill in each key — a friction step that routinely produces misconfiguration.

**Scope**:
- IN: detect-stack.sh output read to determine stack; per-stack default table for Python, Node/JS, Ruby (values derived from Epics A/B routing, not hard-coded separately); stacks without defined defaults (Rust, unknown) write empty string with inline comment explaining why; re-run detection: if key already has a non-empty value, emit [DSO INFO] commands.<key> already set — skipping and preserve; architect-foundation/SKILL.md updated with per-stack table; CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md updated with per-stack table
- OUT: Java stack (Epic F's work); CI skeleton hashFiles() (Story E2); INSTALL.md restructure (Story E3); hard-coding defaults independently of Epics A/B

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, running architect-foundation on a Node/JS project fixture produces a dso-config.conf where all 4 commands.* keys are pre-filled with the Epics A/B defaults for Node/JS (not empty strings)
  ← Satisfies: "architect-foundation pre-fills commands.test_runner, commands.lint, commands.format, commands.format_check for Python, Node/JS, and Ruby stacks"
- When this story is complete, running architect-foundation on a Ruby project fixture produces a dso-config.conf where all 4 commands.* keys are pre-filled with the Epics A/B defaults for Ruby
  ← Satisfies: "defaults derived from Epics A/B routing logic (not hard-coded separately)"
- When this story is complete, running architect-foundation on a Rust or unknown project fixture produces a dso-config.conf where commands.* keys contain empty string with an inline comment explaining no default is defined
  ← Satisfies: "stacks without a defined default (Rust, unknown) write empty string with inline comment"
- When this story is complete, re-running architect-foundation on a project where commands.test_runner is already set to a custom value: (a) emits [DSO INFO] commands.test_runner already set — skipping; (b) does NOT overwrite the custom value
  ← Satisfies: "Re-run preserves existing customized values; emits [DSO INFO] commands.<key> already set — skipping"
- When this story is complete, architect-foundation/SKILL.md and CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md each contain a per-stack reference table enumerating pre-filled keys and default values for Python, Node/JS, Ruby, and Rust
  ← Satisfies: "architect-foundation/SKILL.md and CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md updated with per-stack reference table"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for stack-detection routing (Python/Node/Ruby/Rust/unknown), pre-fill logic, and re-run preservation

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Derive all default values from Epics A/B routing tables at runtime (read their config or documentation) — do not duplicate them; if Epics A/B have not landed, write the current known defaults with a comment noting they should match A/B
- [Reliability] Re-run preservation: read the current dso-config.conf before writing; check each key individually; only write keys that are currently empty or absent
- [Testing] Use fixture directories (with appropriate stack markers like package.json for Node/JS, Gemfile for Ruby) to simulate detect-stack.sh output

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Story complete: all 4 tasks closed, completion-verifier PASS. prefill-config.sh created, docs updated, all 6 unit tests GREEN.
comments.author: Test
